svg.title: Six LLM failure modes mapped to their mechanical controls
svg.desc: A two-column matrix. Left column lists six ways a local coding or reviewing model could fail or cheat: editing a test to pass, letting an interface drift, pulling an unapproved dependency, giving humans and agents different specification copies, rewriting the original specification, or making a fix pass without fixing the bug. Each maps via an arrow to the mechanical control on the right that prevents it: read-only contracts, signature-drift checks, locked package restore, one canonical architecture file, a frozen original specification copy, and cross-model review with a parsed verdict.

g.text: Fix hides bug, not fixes it
